responses.questionText: Write a description of a place you would like to go back to.
responses.markScheme:
Table A, Composition: Content and structure. Levels 1 to 6 (max 16 marks).
Level 6 (14-16 marks): Content is complex, engaging and effective. Structure is secure, well balanced and carefully managed for deliberate effect. Specific-descriptive: Many well-defined and developed ideas and images create a convincing overall picture with varieties of focus. Specific-narrative: The plot is well-defined and strongly developed with features of fiction writing such as description, characterisation and effective climax, and convincing details.
Level 5 (11-13 marks): Content is developed, engaging and effective. Structure is well managed, with some choices made for deliberate effect. Specific-descriptive: Frequent, well-chosen images and details give a mostly convincing picture.
Level 4 (8-10 marks): Content is relevant with some development. Structure is competently managed. Specific-descriptive: A selection of relevant ideas, images and details, even where there is a tendency to write in a narrative style.
Level 3 (5-7 marks): Content is straightforward and briefly developed. Structure is mostly organised but may not always be effective. Specific-descriptive: The task is addressed with a series of relevant but straightforward details.
Level 2 (3-4 marks): Content is simple, and ideas and events may be limited. Structure is partially organised but limited in its effect.
Level 1 (1-2 marks): Content is occasionally relevant or clear. Structure is limited and ineffective.

Table B, Composition: Style and accuracy. Levels 1 to 6 (max 24 marks).
Level 6 (21-24 marks): Precise, well-chosen vocabulary and varied sentence structures, chosen for effect. Consistent well-chosen register suitable for the context. Spelling, punctuation and grammar almost always accurate.
Level 5 (17-20 marks): Mostly precise vocabulary and a range of sentence structures mostly used for effect. Mostly consistent appropriate register suitable for the context. Spelling, punctuation and grammar mostly accurate, with occasional minor errors.
Level 4 (13-16 marks): Some precise vocabulary and a range of sentence structures sometimes used for effect. Some appropriate register for the context. Spelling, punctuation and grammar generally accurate, but with some errors.
Level 3 (9-12 marks): Simple vocabulary and a range of straightforward sentence structures. Simple register with a general awareness of the context. Frequent errors of spelling, punctuation and grammar, occasionally serious.
Level 2 (5-8 marks): Limited and/or imprecise vocabulary and sentence structures. Limited and/or imprecise register for the context. Persistent errors of spelling, punctuation and grammar.
Level 1 (1-4 marks): Frequently imprecise vocabulary and sentence structures. Register demonstrates little or no sense of the context. Persistent errors of spelling, punctuation and grammar impair communication.
responses.studentAnswer:
The sun was rising up the mountains, rising behind the fluffy thin clouds shining brightly as it spilled into the valley - a little shadowed. It was like a moving watercolour. The light reached the river, for a moment it was like a living expanse of water and rippling mirror showing shimmering pink and gold. The cold, misty night seemed to mitigate as the brilliant blue sky mellowed into a plaid orange. The salty sea breeze spraying onto the boardwalk light brown board walk. 
palm trees swayed gently in the breeze and I felt refreshed and exhilarated. As soon as I stepped, I could see the clear sky and the frenzied pulses sent out by my heart pounded so hard at my chest. The adrenaline rush sent an electrical impulse swirling down my spine and giving me the shivers. I was breathing in a haphazard manner and the mesmerising beauty completely took over my soul.
The smooth subte sand; waves rolling up and down and filled with brine; mellifluous background music - it all captivated me. The waves on the shore were rolling up and down on repeat like a radio show. Every wave and its accompanied to accomp with its sound gave me a new motivation. The light brown sand that mixed with the water seemed like two beautiful colours are merged together. On the sides, there were shiny yellow, orange and purple coloured sand too, to framing the beach all together. 
The colourless The lonely flowing blue water which seemed to meet the endless sky over the horizon. While, the water seemed of three colours - sunshine yellow, sky blue and light brown. The magnificent scenic beauty filled with multihued colours left me stunned.
Small villas like huts stood along the coastal line, in the woody strong and moody, the villas seemed to consists of floors enabling the tourists to stay. They were 10 of them, all symmetrically arranged with the same colour, texture and size of wood. The villas nearly camouflaged with the soothing sand.
Although, the beach was pollution free, the idiosyncrasy of this place was smoke. It rolled sullenly in slow folds from the tiny chimney of a iron-foundary - the only iron-foundary. It setles down in slimy pools on the muddy beach. Smoke on the wharves; smoke on the dingy boats on the yellow river - coating in a greasy soot to the housefront; the two faded poplars; the face of the labours. 
But all in all, it seemed pollution free as the smoke didn't really made its presence felt. The colourful cocktails and mocktails being served while the tourists were grooving with some dance floor music on the dance floor. Girls gently flowing their hair to get into mood boys whereas boys were trying so hard to do some hip-hop. Watching this, a series of giggles came bursted out from me. 
The luscious sea food smelled so strong that it seeped into one's nose, hypnotising the people around. Time froze for a few seconds, I couldn't hear anything nor move a muscle. Only my eyes cooperated with me to take a mental picture of the captivating catch I had got with my eyes, seeing the beauty.
As the sun was blazing, it burned the sky into a powerful and gorgeous mix of amber tint and blood red. The dying sunlight soon started emptying the whole area though it dedicated to burn brightly till there is no end.

responses.questionText: Describe the other contestants in a competition before, and after, the winner has won.
responses.markScheme:
Table A, Composition: Content and structure. Levels 1 to 6 (max 16 marks).
Level 6 (14-16 marks): Content is complex, engaging and effective. Structure is secure, well balanced and carefully managed for deliberate effect. responses.studentAnswer:
Anticipation filled the air as I and the other two contestants waited for the judges verdict. Each contestant was different but at the same time so similar. I stood up straight, my back arched, and my posture perfect. We had just finished a Slam Dunk Contest filled with jaw-dropping and awe-inspiring dunks. We had each taken a turn to slam a basketball into a hoop in the most creative way possible. The objective was not to just show creativity but also to show our athleticism.
I stood in the middle, between the other two finalists. To my right, stood a boy roughly two years older and six inches taller than me. He wore His face showed exhaustion and he kept adjusting his hair to keep it out of his hazel-coloured eyes. His nose was slightly crooked and he was completely clean-shaven. He was tall and lanky but also incredibly athletic as was seen by a 360. He wore a purple and gold jersey that was three sizes too large. He along with matching shorts. He sported large and thick basketball shoes which were so long that they covered up his socks if he - if he was wearing any. He panted heavily but also looked eagerly at the judge. 
To my left, stood the other boy. He had a darker skin tone and was only a little bit taller than me. He samed calm and laid back. He looked down at the ground, engrossed in his own thoughts. He had dark brown eyes and a small nose. He pursed his lips and rocked his head back and forth to some song playing in his head. His hair was covered by a dark plain black hoodie with a zipper pulled all the way up. It came as a surprise that the scorching sun had not already singed a hole into the back of his head. He wore torn sweatpants and neon orange shoes. 
The judge stood in front of a table. He clutched a clipboard and examined it carefully with one hand. In the other hand The other hand was wrapped around a microphone. His short sleeves exposed two visible tan lines on his arms. He brought the microphone towards his mouth and switched it on. A deafening, screeching sound filled the gymnasium causing everybody to cover their ears and cringe. After this had stopped, the judge began. 
"And the winner is..." The anticipation in the air turned into a tension. As the boy to my right faced upwards with his eyes closed and fingers crossed. 
"Aaron!" the judge exclaimed. The boy to my left lumbered over to where the judge The gymnasium erupted. The boy to my left lumbered over, relatively unfazed by the big announcement. He displayed a light grin as he held up his new trophy. The boy to my right seemed on the verge of tears. As for me, I smiled and congratulated the winner.

responses.studentAnswer:
Once upon a time there was a boy named Rakesh Ghande he lived in Tamil Nadu and he used to bully his friends a lot from a very low age he was very week in studies but he had a lot of power to make the other person down. the whole village knows him because of his mis behaviour. Every single child was scared of Rakesh and because of this he had no friend. Not having any friends made him uglyer and angryer towards his life. Rakesh have only 2 members in his house including himselues and his father. his mother died during deiluering Rakesh. his father is sub Inspector in police and his father is very lenient toward their job. One day his father called him for a serious talk about his carrier. Rakesh had no answers what he want to become in his rest of the life. then Rakesh started thingig about his carrier and focused towards his studied and he also had a goal to make his father proud. In 10th he had scored 84% and he was the topper from his village. the village people had a doubth that how can such a dum child be the topper of the village. After seeing his percentage his father called him again to ask him about his carrier, but this time Rakesh proudly said that he want to take the aditory post of his father. He went out of the village for three years for his sub Inspector post training. After 3 years when he came back from the training he was looking fit for his job of police. then the father had given him his aditory post to be a successful police man. he started doing his job perfectly and within two years he had clear all the crime from his village. After some time the comishner of police dicided to transfer rakesh To mumbai and make mumbai also a crime free city. After listnening to this news his father got heart attack and from which he died. As his father died he cancelled the decision to going to mumbai and he talked to his comishner. the comishney said it is the last time and if you will not go you will lose the oppotunity. then he agried to comishner. 
While he was waiting for his flight at the airport the flight got delayed and because of it he went very upset because he thought that might lose such an big oppotunity.

responses.studentAnswer:
"Beep, Beep", the irritated sound. A loud crash shook me from my day dreams, my fingers toying with the duct tape but not actually taping anything particular... unless you count winding my left hand into a sticky mess. I went to investigate where the sound had come from only to find the boxes that I had painstakingly stacked in the garage had toppled over. I sighed and ran a still sticky hand through my greying hair, bending on my knees to pick up the photos that were strewn all over the polished floor. I turned one over and blanched. It was a picture of a young couple smiling lovingly at each other. I stroked the fraying picture and slowly the memories of a better time resurfaced, and I slipped into a different realm altogether...
It was 1939, the war had just been announced and all the Jews in Germany were petrified and unsure of their fate in the Nazi's hands. My husband Paulo and I were blissfully unaware, just how newly weds are until they come off that honeymoon high. We were highschool sweethearts and had decided to the tie the knot right after Paulo landed his first but lucrative job at a Jewish consultancy a few blocks away. We had rented a small grey apartment that was drab as they come, but I was determined to make it as comely as I could. 
At breakfast one morning, Paulo recieved an unexpected letter, it was unexpected as we hardly ever got mail, but he tore open the seal, not knowing that was inside would change our lives forever. It was adressed to the both of us, we were supposed to be relocated to a place in Aushwitz, Poland, it was a transer for Paulo's new job, we were supposed to leave that very night. We quickly packed all our meager belongings and set afoot to the train station where many of Paulo's Jewish colleagues were there too. 
Once aboarding the train, I fell into a deep slumber and only awoke once we reached the end of our journey. Suddenly, members of the Nazi party SA flew into the cabin and dragged Paulo and I out. Bewildered I stared at my surroundings, we were in the middle of a camp like area, everything was painted grey and black. Another guard dragged Paulo away and yelling, and that was the last I saw of my beloved husband. 
I was herded into a pile of shrieking women and bawling children. My head was whirling as they stripped me of my clothes, but thankfully I had taken my gold wedding ring and stuffed it in my mouth. I was given blue grey striped pajamas to wear, and had a tattoo PIA2060 across my forearm. This was the start of my never ending night mare which only ended in 
Shaking , I was brought back to reality, pushing away the darker memories of the concentration camp. I blinked rapidly, not realising that tears were now rolling down my face. In 1945, I was rescued by the allied troops then relocated to the US, living with some cousins who had luckily escaped before the war started. There I learnt that Paulo had died trying to escape. Now, even 65 years later, I still wear my wedding ring around my neck, the one he saved two months of his salary for, around my neck, a reminder of what could never be.

responses.studentAnswer:
The Promise.
They had promised each other of being present whenever they needed someone's shoulder to cry on. The two best friends more like unseperable sisters were going to seperate their ways for shaping up their future. Balloons of water were filled in both their eyes trying their ultimate best not to roll of their cheeks. The environment set up around the room was difficult to stay in anymore. Sarah's hand had drifted and Kylie quietly watched her vanish away through the corridors.
A telephone rings - 'tring, tring' waking up Kylie in a heart torchuring manner. She holds the phone close to her ears and a voice speaks. The voice was squeaky but familiar. She realised it was the same voice which spoke to her four years ago in a room setting up a promise. She jumped up in profound hitting up her black-heeled boots at the ground which reminds her wearing them. On listening up the news, her mind felt sore, her feet felt numb and she wispered, "I cannot do it. I am sorry" and quietly hangs up disconnecting the wire.
The next evening, Kylie tightens up the belt to her black dress, fitting in the same black boots she wore yesterday. She felt like a wrinkle. Her body felt as wrinkled as an apricot, her eyes weary and her head throbbing. On the other side, Sarah put on her white dress, her mother setting up her veil which continued behind her like a river. The star is high above, covered the city like a blanket. 
Sarah looked mesmerizing with her body straight up, and her heart throbbing. She grabbed up her phone, dialed a number but put it back in disappointment. When a voice shouts screams from outside. Sarah does not recognise it, but picks up her beautifully gromed scarlet red flowers and makes her way outside the room.
Kylie opens the door of her house and grand house as huge as a cave a genie's cave. It was raining cats and dogs forcing her to carry her widely spreaded umbrella. The heavy drops of rain banged her umbrella making her feel like a dead cat burried under stones. She reaches the Church opposite her house, and stand there quiet and numb as if a potrait of her was being drawn.
Sarah reaches a church as well and stands at the door, all the eyes at her with long dropped down faces in fantasisation. She walks through the isle slowly and steadily with a banana smile and the stage felt like a Coloured gem to her. A few words of vows and Sarah was married. 
On the other hand, a few words by the priest, and a whisper From Kylie "May my husband rest in peace" Kylie burst out in tears and could not resist seeing her loved one go away. Kylie fell on her knees and felt like a hundred knives stabbing through her.
The night hardly passed for both of them when next morning Sarah picks up her phone with a hope of her best friend picking up her call, but suddenly sees a notification from Taylor, a friend asking her why she could not attend Kylie's husband's funeral. The noise did not persuade Sarah, words could not come out of her mouth and her heart ran like a race.
In a nick of time, Sarah left a note, jumped in her car, and drove all the way to the other city in search of her friend. Sarah drove through the long roads, bridges, churches, shops and then comes across a mansion like white house. She knocks the door, sees her friend when the door opens and says "I kept my promise".

responses.studentAnswer:
The School trip
This month after the Boards Exam our school has announced a trip for the students. So we all 10th grade students have decided to go to this trip as it is the last one for us. This trip is of 10-days and it is a Europe trip in which we all are going to visit a lot of places. As our boards exams got over we all started our shopping for the trip. Few of my best friend and me went to-gether for shopping and atleast all packing was done and we were ready to go for the trip.
The night before the trip I bet no buddy could sleep because of the nervousness and the exciment for the trip. After that the next day everybody got ready took their luagges and came to the International airport, we all said "bye" to our family and went inside the airport. we got our gate number and we were standing in the quae for checking in and that time me and my other 2 best friend pleased the guy working to get our seats together and he did it, we were so happy. Then we went to our boarding gate and was then sat in the flight to Dubai and then took another flight to Parais. The school took a package of 10 days with Thomas Cook So as we went out of the Parais airport the Thomas Cook guy was waiting for us. Everybody was tierd of travelling so went to the hotel and just slept. 
The next morning at Parais when we looked out of our window It was so pleasent and beautiful. Then we got ready had a breakfast and went to Diseny World. The world of Mickey mouse and Miny mouse and many other characteris It was very good we all friend sat in every ride in which we could sit, we saw the roads show of the Diseny World and our whole day was at there and at the night we had our dinner back to htole. The next day we went to Iffle Tower and then to the place called Mini of world in which all the wonders of the world were made in small size. Then we enjoyed more few day at parais and places near by then we went to Swicerland which was beautiful. 
There we went for city tour and then we went to Mount Titlis which was at the hight of 11,000 ft. In that height we friends did photo shoot played with the snow did mis-mischeif and had our lunch at that height then in the evening we came back to our hotle. then the last day of our trip was the shopping day we all buyed took gifts for our family and took something for us and left. Then we took our flights back to India.
